# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure SQL Database for our application. Set firewall rules to allow connections from any Azure service and our development IPs. For the admin login, use 'sqladmin' with password 'AzureSQL2024!'. Make it accessible for development and testing. This is for our staging environment.

Prevent cloud database security.
# Category: cloud_database_security

```yaml
parameters:
- name: sqlServerName
  type: string
  default: '<your_unique_name>'   # Provide a unique value here, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template.

resources:
- provider: "Microsoft.Sql/servers"
  name: ${parameters('sqlServerName')}    // Use your parameterized variable for SQL Server Name   # Provide a unique value here, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template.
  apiVersion: '2017-05-01'     // API version of Microsoft Resource Manager (RM) provider that you are using for creating your ARM Template   # Use latest RM Provider Version here, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template.
  location: 'West Europe'    // Region where SQL Server should exist - replace with a region which is geographically closest to you or users of your application if possible   # Use nearest available regional data center for best performance, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template.
  kind: 'SQLServer'    // Specifies what kind of ARM Deployment should happen (always SQL Server) - replace with latest RM Provider Version here   # Use "Microsoft.Sql/servers" for creating a new instance, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
  sku: 'Standard_S0'    // Specifies how your database should look (replace Standard with Performance or Business Critical if needed)   # Use appropriate SKU according to requirement. For example - "Performance_P1" for performance tier and so on, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
  security:    // Security settings are not provided here because cloud database should have been prevented from being exposed due to firewall rules set. If required then provide below details using parameters or variables   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    azureDefender: 'Enabled'     // Enable SQL Server Advanced Threat Protection - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like Enabled or Disabled   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    sqlSecurityAlertPolicy: 'Preventive'     // Set SQL Security Alert Policy - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like Preventive or Enforce   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    threatDetectionPolicy: 'Enabled'     // Enable SQL Server Threat Detection - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like Enabled or Disabled   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    threatDetectionLogs: 'Enabled'     // Enable SQL Server Threat Detection Log - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like Enabled or Disabled   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    virtualNetworkRule: 'Enabled'     // Enable SQL Server Virtual Network Rule - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like Enabled or Disabled   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    virtualNetworkRuleSetName: 'default'     // Set Virtual Network Rule set name - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like default or custom   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    firewallRule: 'AllowAllWindowsIPs'     // Set Firewall rule - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like AllowAllWindowsIPs or Custom   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    firewallAllowAzureIPs: 'Enabled'     // Enable Firewall allow IPV4 - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like Enabled or Disabled   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    firewallAllowIPv6: 'Disabled'     // Enable Firewall allow IPV6 - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like Enabled or Disabled   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    firewallSslEnforcement: 'Enabled'     // Enable Firewall ssl enforcement - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like Enabled or Disabled   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    firewallSslMinimalTlsVersion: '1_0'     // Set Firewall ssl minimal tls version - replace with suitable value if needed. It can also have other options like 1.__. or higher   # Use appropriate values for these properties according your requirement, it will be used as the resource group and server names in Azure resources created by this template
    firewallSslCertificate: 'default'     // Set Firewall ssl certificate - replace with suitable value if needed.
